content.full_text:
OFFICE OF THE COUNTY COUNSEL
COUNTY OF SUTTER
1160 Civic Center Boulevard
Yuba City, California 95993

DARRELL W. LARSEN
County Counsel

Telephone (916) 741-7110

RONALD S. ERICKSON
Assistant

JAMES SCANLON
JOSEPH P. CERULLO
DENNISE LE CLERE
Deputies

October 16, 1992

Mr. Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

RE: REQUEST FOR ADVICE

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

We are requesting this advice on behalf of Sutter County Supervisor
Ronald Southard. Sutter County has adopted a PUBLIC SEWER AND
WATER POLICY for residential development in a portion of the
unincorporated area of the County near the City of Yuba City which
provides in part the following:

"Prior to approval of a master plan for sewer and water
services, the County will only approve new tentative maps
which include immediate connection to public sewer and
water services."

A developer has filed with the County an application for
subdividing approximately 3.39 acres ("the subdivision") into six
residential units within the area covered by the above-described
policy. Supervisor Southard resides on a parcel, the nearest point
to the subdivision being his southeast corner which is
approximately 910 feet from the northwest corner of the
subdivision. Supervisor Southard's direct interest in his
residential property exceeds one thousand dollars.

We are requesting advice as to whether or not Supervisor Southard
may participate and vote on the developer's application for an
exception to the above-described policy and in particular for
permission to subdivide the property without the necessity of
hooking the parcels up to public sewer service. We make this
request for advice pursuant to and as contemplated by Government
Code section 83114.

Very truly yours,

DARRELL W. LARSEN
SUTTER COUNTY COUNSEL

JAMES SCANLON
DEPUTY COUNTY COUNSEL

JS:js

CC: Supervisor Ronald Southard

sections.question: We are requesting advice as to whether or not supervisor southard may participate and vote on the developer's application for an exception to the above-described policy and in particular for permission to subdivide the property without the necessity of hooking the parcels up to public sewer service.
sections.question_synthetic: May Supervisor Southard participate and vote on a developer's application for a subdivision exemption from the county's public sewer and water policy when his residential property, located approximately 910 feet away, would be affected by the decision?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from County Counsel to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion section present. Document contains specific factual details about the conflict of interest situation and cites Government Code section 83114 as the basis for the request.

embedding.summary: Sutter County Counsel requests FPPC advice regarding whether Supervisor Ronald Southard may participate in voting on a developer's exemption request from the county's public sewer and water service policy, given Southard's nearby residential property interest exceeding $1,000.
